data.title: Walmart stock trades in the green for seven consecutive days
data.description:
Walmart (NYSE:WMT) shares traded in the green on Thursday, making it the seventh consecutive day of gains. The stock closed up 0.90% at $95.30.

WMT rose 84% in the last 12 months. YTD, the stock is trading up 81.35%, compared to the broader S&P 500 market which is up 27.37%.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, this company has a Hold rating with a score of 3.48 out of 5.

When it comes to Wall Street analysis, 25 out of 42 analysts recommend a Strong Buy, 13 recommend a Buy, 3 recommend it as a Hold and 1 recommended Strong Sell.

According to SA analyst Rob Barnett, “Walmart's international division is key to growth, focusing on high-growth markets like Mexico and China, while US growth aligns with the economy.

Operating margins have declined but show signs of stabilization; international margins are improving due to restructuring.”

Seeking Alpha analysts have a Hold rating on the company.

data.title: OPEC+ production delay buys time but could face similar dilemma in three months
data.description:
Crude oil futures ticked lower Thursday following the well-telegraphed move by OPEC+ to extend production caps at current levels for another three months until April, but barrels would still be added next year to a market that is expected to be oversupplied.

The cartel agreed to extend voluntary production cuts of 2.2M bbl/day until the end of March, with the full amount set to be returned to the market over 18 months until the end of September 2026.

The gradual rollback of voluntary curbs, which was originally set to start in October and run for 12 months through September 2025, already had been delayed twice.

The OPEC+ decision shows the group is concerned about a potential supply glut and lack of compliance with targets among some member countries, Rystad Energy's Mukesh Sahdev said, estimating the latest plan will lower expected 2025 supply by 1.03M bbl/day, turning a crude surplus of 700K bbl/day into a deficit of 300K bbl/day.

"The overall signal to the market is constructive and will likely prevent any price downsides in the short term," Sahdev wrote.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for January delivery closed -0.3% to $68.30/bbl, and front-month February Brent crude (CO1:COM) also finished -0.3% to $72.09/bbl, while front-month January Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) settled +1.2% to $3.079/MMBtu after the EIA reported U.S. supplies declined by 30B cf for the week ended November 29.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

But some analysts pointed to a plentiful supply outlook for 2025 as offsetting support from the OPEC+ decision.

The move "buys the group some time," David Oxley of Capital Economics said, but "the backdrop of weak global oil demand means that it could easily find itself back in a similar position in three months' time."

The delay will tighten the global oil market by ~0.5% of demand and support oil prices at the margin, Oxley said, and "we now judge that the oil market will be roughly in balance across 2025 as a whole, compared to our previous forecast of a small surplus."

"The market is facing a surplus, there is no shortage of oil and there is not really any flashing sign of what to look forward to in the future to rally prices," Mizuho's Bob Yawger said.

"A delay won't cut it," TD Commodities strategist Daniel Ghali wrote, and while OPEC's plan will keep a floor under prices in the near term, "the drag from energy supply risk premia will persist nonetheless."

data.title: Baxter restarts dialysis solutions production at North Cove plant
data.description:
Baxter International (NYSE:BAX) said it has re-started all of its peritoneal dialysis solutions manufacturing lines at its North Cove, N.C., plant, which was knocked offline by flooding from Hurricane Helene in late September.

“While we currently expect that all available lines will be restarted by the end of the year, we do not yet have a specific date for when we expect North Cove production to be fully restored to pre-hurricane levels,” Baxter said in an updated posted on its website.

The plant is the nation’s largest producer of intravenous and peritoneal dialysis solutions. The temporary closure of the plant has resulted in Baxter (BAX) employing a variety of inventory management and allocation measures to help minimize disruptions to patient care.

The company noted in its update on Thursday that two of the restarted IV solutions lines were now producing 1-liter IV solutions, the most commonly used size by hospitals, at around 85% of the site's pre-hurricane capacity.

data.title: Zumiez swings to a profit as footwear and apparel outperform
data.description:
Shares of Zumiez (NASDAQ:ZUMZ) are rallying in Thursday’s after-hours as the company reported a 7.5% increase in comparable sales, which lifted net sales by 3% during Q3 and swung the company to a profit of $0.06 per share from a loss of $0.12 in the same quarter last year.

“The strategic initiatives we have been executing this year have contributed to a noticeable increase in sales trends. Fueled by growing strength in our North American business, consolidated comparable sales sequentially accelerated 390 basis points to high-single digits in the third quarter. We experienced strong gains in our apparel and footwear categories as consumers have responded favorably to our refined product assortments and enhanced customer engagement tactics,” said CEO Rick Brooks.

For the current quarter, the specialty retailer of apparel, footwear, equipment and accessories expects sales to be in the range of $284M to $288M, topping estimates for $283.5M in sales. Earnings, however, came in shy of expectations with a range of $0.83 to $0.93 per share versus the $1.01 estimate. Comparable sales are expected to increase by 6% to 7.5%.

Shares are up 7% after-hours.

data.title: Platinum Group Metals enters into at-the-market equity distribution agreement
data.description:
• Platinum Group Metals (NYSE:PLG) on Thursday said it has entered into a new equity distribution agreement effective as of December 5 for a new at-the-market equity program.
  • The agreement will allow the company to distribute up to $50 million of common shares of the company under the 2024 ATM Program.
  • The net proceeds of any such sales under the 2024 ATM Program are anticipated to be used, over the following 24 months, for the Waterberg property pre-construction site work, engineering and preparation, a potential phase one development program at the Waterberg Project, a Saudi Arabia smelter and base metal refinery definitive feasibility study, a contingency provision and general, corporate and administrative expenses.
  • PLG +0.62% after hours to $1.61.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Robinhood now a 'best in class top and bottom-asset under custody compounder': Goldman
Gold may hit new record next year as Fed reduces rates, Macquarie says
data.description:
Gold futures fell Thursday as U.S. Treasury yields firmed after the release of weekly jobless claims data, while markets await U.S. non-farm payrolls data on Friday for more cues into the Federal Reserve's next move on potential interest rate cuts.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell said the economy looks better now than it did in September, which means the central bank can move more slowly in reducing borrowing costs.

Investor focus now shifts to Friday's U.S. non-farm payrolls, which are forecast to increase by 200K jobs in November after rising by only 12K in October, for further clarity on the interest rate path.

A strong result is more or less priced in, but any weakness in the report could add some support to gold prices, Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen said.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for December delivery closed -1% to $2,626.60/oz, the lowest settlement value since November 26, and front-month December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -1.2% to $31.13/oz, as both metals broke two-session winning streaks.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Heading into 2025, some analysts think gold can resume its upward climb, as the Federal Reserve cuts rates and central banks add bullion to reserves.

Analysts at Macquarie think gold may struggle in next year's Q1 due to a strengthening dollar, but it could extend gains after that, and perhaps "quickly challenge" $3,000/oz if Chinese demand picks up, or if President-elect Trump's policies risk a deterioration in the U.S. fiscal outlook.

"Our base case into 2025 is for gold to initially face ongoing pressure from U.S. dollar strength but be supported by improved physical buying and steady official sector demand," according to Macquarie analysts including Marcus Garvey. "Thereafter, we expect it to receive another investor boost as the FOMC bring rates down towards 4%."

ETF holdings are still 25% below their 2020 high, "suggesting plenty of scope" for more purchases if conditions justify it, Macquarie said. "In this case we view falling interest rates as key, by way of reducing the appeal of money market funds and other savings products."

data.title: Renegade Gold announces $1M flow-through private placement
data.description:
• Renegade Gold (TSXV:RAGE:CA) announced on Thursday that it proposes to undertake a non-brokered private placement of flow-through common shares at a price of $0.18 per FT Share for gross proceeds of up to $1 million.
  • The company intends to use the gross proceeds to incur "Canadian exploration expenses" that are "flow-through mining expenditures" related to the company's projects in Ontario.
  • In particular, the gross proceeds will be used to advance the company's exploration program in the highly prospective Red Lake District, Ontario.

data.title: OSI Systems names Ajay Mehra as president and CEO
data.description:
• OSI Systems (NASDAQ:OSIS) has appointed Ajay Mehra, current president of OSI’s Security division and executive vice president of OSI, as the company’s new president and chief executive officer, effective January 1, 2025.
  • Mehra will succeed Deepak Chopra, who will transition to executive chairman of the board.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Petco posts results beat for Q3, guides slightly lower for current quarter; shares rise
data.description:
Petco Health and Wellness (NASDAQ:WOOF) posted a results beat on all major metrics for the third quarter, which helped offset the slightly lower-than-expected current quarter forecast.

Shares of the animal wellness products retailer rose more than 12% and retreated below 10% in post-market trading on Thursday.

The company now anticipates fourth quarter net revenue to be about $1.55B, which is below the consensus estimate of $1.57B. Adjusted earnings per share is expected to be between naught and 2 cents. The consensus estimate is 4 cents.

"Growth in the company's consumables and services and other businesses was offset by the company's supplies and companion animal business, down 2.8 percent versus prior year," Petco said in a statement.

In Q3, comparable sales rose 1.8%, and adjusted EBITDA was $9M higher from last year at $81.2M.

Net loss attributable to shareholders for the quarter came down to $16.7M, or $0.06 loss per share, from $1.2B, or $4.63 loss per share, for the same period a year earlier. The year-ago period included a $1.2B non-cash goodwill impairment charge.

On an adjusted per-share basis, the company lost 2 cents, while analysts, on average, were expecting a 3-cent loss.

Revenue rose 1.3% to $1.51B and beat the consensus estimates.
